name: typeorm
description: TypeORM CLI for managing database entities, migrations, subscribers, and schema operations across multiple database drivers

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Initialize a new TypeORM project with a predefined directory structure, sample entity, migration, and ormconfig file

options.name: --name
options.description: Name of the project directory to create

options.name: --database
options.description: Database type (postgres, mysql, mariadb, sqlite, mssql, oracle, cockroachdb, mongodb)

options.name: --express
options.description: Generate an Express application with TypeORM integration

options.name: --docker
options.description: Generate docker-compose.yml for the selected database

options.name: --pm
options.description: Package manager to use: npm, yarn, or pnpm

subcommands.name: migration:create
subcommands.description: Create a new empty migration file with up() and down() methods. The file is timestamped and placed in the configured migrations directory.

args.name: path
args.description: Path and name for the migration file (e.g., src/migrations/CreateUsersTable)

options.name: --outputJs
options.description: Generate migration file in JavaScript instead of TypeScript

options.name: --timestamp
options.description: Custom timestamp for the migration file

subcommands.name: migration:generate
subcommands.description: Automatically generate a migration by comparing the current entity definitions to the current database schema. Detects added/removed columns, tables, and relations.

args.name: path
args.description: Path and name for the generated migration file

options.name: --dataSource
options.description: Path to the data source configuration file

options.name: --pretty
options.description: Pretty-print the generated SQL queries

options.name: --outputJs
options.description: Generate migration file in JavaScript instead of TypeScript

options.name: --dryrun
options.description: Print the generated migration SQL without writing files

options.name: --check
options.description: Exit with error code if there are pending schema changes

subcommands.name: migration:run
subcommands.description: Run all pending migrations that have not yet been applied to the database. Executes migrations in chronological order.

options.name: --dataSource
options.description: Path to the data source configuration file

options.name: --transaction
options.description: Transaction mode: all, each, or none

options.name: --fake
options.description: Mark migrations as run without actually executing them

subcommands.name: migration:revert
subcommands.description: Revert the most recently executed migration. Run multiple times to revert multiple migrations in reverse order.

options.name: --dataSource
options.description: Path to the data source configuration file

options.name: --transaction
options.description: Transaction mode: all, each, or none

options.name: --fake
options.description: Mark migration as reverted without actually executing the down method

subcommands.name: migration:show
subcommands.description: Show all migrations and their status (pending or applied) for the configured database connection.

options.name: --dataSource
options.description: Path to the data source configuration file

subcommands.name: entity:create
subcommands.description: Create a new empty entity class file with the @Entity() decorator and a primary generated column.

args.name: path
args.description: Path and name for the entity file (e.g., src/entities/User)

options.name: --outputJs
options.description: Generate entity file in JavaScript instead of TypeScript

subcommands.name: subscriber:create
subcommands.description: Create a new subscriber class file that listens to entity events like insert, update, and remove.

args.name: path
args.description: Path and name for the subscriber file

options.name: --outputJs
options.description: Generate subscriber file in JavaScript instead of TypeScript

subcommands.name: schema:drop
subcommands.description: Drop all tables and their data from the database. WARNING: This is a destructive operation.

options.name: --dataSource
options.description: Path to the data source configuration file

subcommands.name: schema:log
subcommands.description: Show the SQL statements that would be executed to synchronize the database schema with the current entity definitions, without applying them.

options.name: --dataSource
options.description: Path to the data source configuration file

subcommands.name: schema:sync
subcommands.description: Synchronize the database schema based on current entity definitions. Creates and alters tables to match entities. Not recommended for production.

options.name: --dataSource
options.description: Path to the data source configuration file

subcommands.name: cache:clear
subcommands.description: Clear all data stored in the query result cache configured for the connection.

options.name: --dataSource
options.description: Path to the data source configuration file

subcommands.name: query
subcommands.description: Execute a raw SQL query against the configured database and display the results.

options.name: --dataSource
options.description: Path to the data source configuration file

examples: typeorm query "SELECT * FROM users LIMIT 10" -d src/data-source.ts
typeorm-ts-node-commonjs query "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders" -d src/data-source.ts

subcommands.name: version
subcommands.description: Display installed versions of TypeORM, database drivers, and related packages.

globalOptions.name: --dataSource
globalOptions.description: Path to the TypeORM DataSource file

examples: npx typeorm query "SELECT * FROM users" -d src/data-source.ts

detectors.name: typeorm_datasource_detect
detectors.description: Detect TypeORM data source configuration files

detectors.name: typeorm_entity_detect
detectors.description: Detect TypeORM entity files in the project

detectors.name: typeorm_version
detectors.command: npx typeorm version 2>/dev/null | head -5 || echo 'not installed'
